comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Advisory triage on re-review PASS (0 blocking, 8 advisory). adv1/3/4/5 all restate the SAME accepted scope boundary now written into the ACs: HmacScheme is a forward-looking first-class HMAC-over-DSSE-PAE registry scheme; real legacy plan-review/completion-verifier records (canonical-payload + hex sig) are NOT rerouted through the registry and keep verifying UNCHANGED via the untouched signing.py path (expand phase; re-enveloping old records is the op-cert epic contract phase). This is an epic-level decision, justified-not-defect. adv6/7/8 (impact 0.0): the depends_on 518b is encoded by the review stack ordering (518b lands below 7e53). Proceeding to TDD.

compiled_state.description:
## Context
Bring the symmetric HMAC-SHA256 primitive (`src/rebar/signing.py`) into the attest scheme registry (`src/rebar/attest/registry.py`, task 518b) as a **first-class registry scheme** so the substrate acknowledges HMAC and both consumer epics reference one registry. This is the **expand** phase.

**Scope boundary (deliberate).** Existing plan-review / completion-verifier attestations are stored in the *legacy* shape: a hex HMAC over the canonical `{v,algorithm,ticket_id,manifest}` payload, in the record `signature` field — NOT a DSSE envelope. This leaf does **NOT** reroute those old records through the registry; they keep verifying **unchanged** through the untouched `signing.py` path. What this leaf adds is (a) an `HmacScheme` registered in the registry (so HMAC is a known scheme, keyed by its algorithm id) and (b) `POLICY` entries pinning the legacy kinds to it. The HMAC->asymmetric cutover and any re-enveloping of old records is the op-cert epic contract phase, out of scope here.

## Contract (concrete)
- `HmacScheme` implements the registry `Scheme` protocol; `name = signing.ALGORITHM` (`"HMAC-SHA256"`).
- `HmacScheme.verify(pae_bytes, signatures, namespace, trust_root) -> registry.Verdict` computes HMAC-SHA256 over `pae_bytes` (the DSSE-PAE bytes) using the key supplied as `trust_root` and constant-time-compares (`hmac.compare_digest`) to `signatures[0].sig` (the raw bytes of the first `dsse.Signature`). Result: match -> `Verdict(True,"certified",...)`; non-match -> `Verdict(False,"mismatch",...)`; empty/missing key -> fail closed `Verdict(False,"foreign_key",...)`; no signatures -> `Verdict(False,"mismatch",...)`.
- `register_legacy_schemes()` is **idempotent**: registers the `HmacScheme` and pins `POLICY["plan-review"]` and `POLICY["completion-verifier"]` to `Policy(scheme="HMAC-SHA256", namespace="rebar-attest-hmac")`. Called once from `src/rebar/attest/__init__.py` at import.

## Files
- `src/rebar/attest/hmac_legacy.py` — `HmacScheme` + `register_legacy_schemes()`.
- `src/rebar/attest/__init__.py` — call `register_legacy_schemes()` at import.
- `src/rebar/signing.py` — **UNCHANGED** (regression guard).
- `tests/unit/test_attest_hmac_legacy.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After import, `registry.get_scheme("HMAC-SHA256")` returns the `HmacScheme`, and `registry.resolve("plan-review").scheme == "HMAC-SHA256"` and `registry.resolve("completion-verifier").scheme == "HMAC-SHA256"` (both pinned to namespace `rebar-attest-hmac`) — test asserts all four.
- [ ] `HmacScheme.verify` fail-closed matrix over DSSE-PAE bytes: correct key -> `certified`; wrong key -> `mismatch`; empty key -> `foreign_key`; empty signatures list -> not certified. A round-trip helper builds `signatures[0].sig = hmac_sha256(key, pae_bytes)` to prove the certified path.
- [ ] `register_legacy_schemes()` is idempotent — calling it twice leaves exactly one `HMAC-SHA256` scheme and the same two POLICY entries (test).
- [ ] Regression / no-behavior-change: a plan-review manifest signed via `signing.sign_manifest` still returns `verdict == "certified"` from `signing.verify_signature`, and `src/rebar/signing.py` sign/verify functions are not modified by this change (test signs+verifies exactly as before and gets `certified`).

file_impact.reason: register built-in legacy schemes at import
registration, HMAC-over-PAE fail-closed matrix, signing.py regression

compiled_state.title: attest: register existing HMAC as a legacy scheme
